title: SyncEnvelope
description: The bidirectional delta-sync envelope between a node/app and the cloud. White paper §5, §10; ADR-007. Carries only records changed since the last cursor. Idempotent apply: a replayed envelope is a no-op.

since_cursor.description: Opaque cursor from the last successful sync; null for a full initial pull.

next_cursor.description: Returned by the server on pull; the client stores it for next time.

priority.description: accountability lane (ledger/issuance/attendance/audit) is flushed first to minimise single-copy exposure (§11).

items.description: Batch of changed records, each tagged with its type and idempotency key.

revision.description: Idempotency key. Server applies once per {id, revision}; replays return idempotency_replay, not a double-post.

payload.description: The record, conforming to the schema named by `type`.

conflicts.description: On pull, true conflicts the server resolved (e.g. marks cloud-wins). The client surfaces these to the owning teacher; never silently dropped.
